title: Marsdenia minima
taxonomy: Marsdenia minima is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Marsdenia, which is a group of tropical vines and shrubs. The family Apocynaceae is a large and diverse group of plants that also includes milkweeds and oleander. Members of the Apocynaceae family are typically characterized by their showy flowers and milky sap. Marsdenia minima is native to Australia and is often found in rocky areas.

intro: Marsdenia minima, also known as the dwarf milkvine, is a perennial climber of the Apocynaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate various health conditions such as fever, cough, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Marsdenia minima include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
